summary: Titles in file manager truncated with ellipses despite new horizontal scroll bar.

description.text: ### Description I use the file manager's "thin view" and noticed that the file manager now adds a horizontal scroll bar to allow users to view long titles that overflow the width of the sidebar. The trouble is that when I drag one of these scrollbars to see the portion of the title that's overflowing, I find that the title text has been truncated with ellipses, which are placed in the spot where the title presumably would otherwise have run "off the page" in the absence of the scrollbar: ### Reproducing Set the file manager view to `Thin view` in preferences. Create or rename a file with a long title that overflows the width of the file manager's sidebar (or simply shrink the width of the file manager sidebar until it is narrower than existing file titles). When the horizontal scrollbar appears beneath the long title, drag it to the right. ### Zettlr version 4.0.0-beta-nightly ### Installation Method From the Website or GitHub ### Installation Method (Other) AppImage ### Your Platform - [ ] Windows - [ ] macOS - [x] Linux ### Architecture - [x] Intel 64bit - [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon) ### Operating System Version Manjaro (Rolling) ### Additional Information Thanks, as always!

## Description
<!-- Below, please describe what the PR does in one or two short sentences. -->

## Changes
<!-- What changes did you make? Please explicitly state any breaking API changes so that nobody is confused why other components suddenly stop working -->
This PR adds a checkbox toggle to the Export popover which allows a user to control whether the exported document is automatically opened. It also saves the last selected export profile per file.

Closes #5930
Closes #6002

## Additional information
<!-- If there is anything else that might be of interest, please provide it here -->
A checkbox was added to the Export popover bound to the `export.autoOpenExportedFiles` config value.

The label for the `Format` element was moved to a translation.

<!-- Please provide any testing system -->
Tested on: <!-- OS including version --> macOS 26

summary: Update check displays an error dialog when connection failed

description.text:
### Description

Hello,

when I'm connected to my company VPN, Zettlr displays a dialog announcing that it Can not check for update: "There was an error while checking for updates. ETIMEDOUT: Could not check for updates. TimeoutError: Timeout awaiting 'request' for 5000ms".

Could you prevent displaying the dialog when the check is done on a background and is not requested manually? 

### Reproducing

I suppose the issue is caused by internet connection limit - the update site is not available when I'm connected to VPN. Try to request update when the computer has no access to the internet.

### Zettlr version

3.6.0

### Installation Method

Homebrew

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [ ] Intel 64bit
- [x] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Sequoia 15.7.1

### Additional Information

_No response_

## Description This PR refactors the styling of the assets window. ## Changes Elements had margin spacing increased so they are no longer cramped together. The `Open Directory` button of the defaults and snippets tab was moved to below the file picker. The file picker element, `SelectableList` was made so that it expands to the size of the container, and when the window shrinks or there are too many files, the list scrolls rather than the whole container. See the attached video. Because of this change, the Preferences window had to be adjusted so that it scrolls correctly. The `CSS` tab was moved to the end of the list because it offers a different window setup than snippets and defaults, which share a structure. The `Save` button labeling was refactored to add some delay when clicking save so that the label doesn't quickly flash between `Saving...` and `Saved!`. ## Additional information **Current:** https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8217c79c-559d-4db7-9b64-2816519e870b **PR:** https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/50fb9bcb-0d0c-48b4-a35b-953909343823 Tested on: macOS 26 and Fedora 42

CC @kyaso and @benniekiss 

I have a quest that we should fix before releasing the next stable version. I feel the table editor works very well and great by now. But there is one final remaining bug that I can't for the love of god pin down. That's why I would like to ask you two to also take a look and think about this. Maybe you can find the error in my thinking. You have decent experience with the inner workings of the app, and I would love to get your help for fixing this remaining issue!

Here's the problem: For large-enough tables, the editor will sometimes violently jump around, and it is very annoying when trying to editing tables, or even just the text next to it. This happens also when no change is being performed other than, e.g., saving a file.

Here's what I've observed so far:

1. This does not happen when no table cell wraps around, that is, if the contents of all table cells are small enough to fit into a single line.
2. The more wrapping occurs (= content of single table cells spanning multiple visual lines), the more violent the jumping behavior becomes
3. This becomes worse if the table is large enough to fill the entire viewport. As a reminder: CodeMirror has a concept of a viewport, and will gracefully display and remove lines one by one as you scroll through a long document. It uses a placeholder element with a manually set height for every element that it doesn't display to maintain the scroll height. I've noticed that jumping is extreme when there is a table that is high enough that CodeMirror will ONLY render the table itself, plus two placeholder elements for before and after the table.
4. What I have noticed in this regard is that, if you click inside a table with wrapped cells when CodeMirror only displays the table and two placeholder elements, CodeMirror will suddenly re-render some elements below the table widget, jump downwards, and once you manually scroll back, it will correctly un-render those elements again.
5. After a few scroll jumps, editing the table cell becomes possible again. It's almost as if the editor "recovers."
6. Lastly, I have also noticed, that, when the jumping occurs, there will occasionally be warnings that the "viewport did not stabilize" after a few attempts.

It would be great if you could also give it a shot and think about how we might fix this.

Here's a quick way to reproduce:

1. Create a table that contains enough rows to fill the viewport.
2. Ensure that the table gets squeezed (e.g., by opening the devtools and making it wide) so that the cells wrap.
3. Ensure that there are a few paragraphs/lines/elements above and below the table to observe the sudden re-rendering.
4. Click into various cells and observe the jumping behavior.

Thank you already in advance!

summary: Directory > Properties > Sort by time: does not auto refresh

description.text:
### Description

In the ideal situation the working repository sidebar folder view with `Properties` set to `Sort by time` would automatically update.

e.g. saving a file would move it to the top if `Sort by time` and `ascending` is selected in `Properties`.

Instead, the order does not refresh until right-click repository in working area to bring up `Properties` dialogue. 

### Reproducing

Save new version of file.

File does not automatically change its timed sort order in `Repositories` sidebar folder view.

### Zettlr version

3.6.0

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Windows 11 Pro 24H2

### Additional Information

_No response_

## Description
<!-- Below, please describe what the PR does in one or two short sentences. -->
This PR fixes two bugs related to tabstop removal from snippets 

Closes #5933

## Changes
<!-- What changes did you make? Please explicitly state any breaking API changes so that nobody is confused why other components suddenly stop working -->
The `Escape` shortcut was fixed so that it now correctly removes all remaining tabstops. There was ony a small typo.

Tabstops are now removed if the context before them was deleted by mapping positions using `MapMode.TrackBefore`. This enables a user to manually select and remove tabstops.

## Additional information
<!-- If there is anything else that might be of interest, please provide it here -->

<!-- Please provide any testing system -->
Tested on: <!-- OS including version --> macOS 26

summary: Manually deleting a snippet does not remove the tabstop

description.text:
### Description

When a pending snippet is deleted with remaining tabstops, the tabstops remain visible and active in the editor. Continuing to type, then pressing tab, will result in the cursor returning to the tabstop.

### Reproducing

Create a snippet with at least one tabstop: `Test $1`

Enter the snippet into the editor.

Select the snippet text and delete it. The tabstop remains.

### Zettlr version

Develop branch

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [ ] Intel 64bit
- [x] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

macOS 26

### Additional Information

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/90f28535-0b5e-4185-b13d-4c67f4bd7e1b

### Description Very simply, when one types `![](png.png)`, assuming this png exists, it will render it. Further, the png is shown in the "Other files" tab. For webp files, `![](webp.webp)` does not render, as in issue #5181. However, I can get it to render by specifying a relative path. Therefore, `![](./webp.webp)` will render. The following markdown ``` ![](webp.webp) ![](./webp.webp) ![](png.png) ``` Produces: using webp.webp and png.png files attached. ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/388e9727-6216-4727-ae7e-7c88dfd1888f) There are three things I expect to happen: 1. ![](webp.webp) renders the image. 2. webp.webp appears in the "Other files" tab 3. Clicking and dragging webp.webp into the markdown file from explorer adds the image to the document. None of these are currently working. ### Reproducing 1. Create workspace. 2. Put webp.webp and png.png into a subdirectory (or just in the main workspace). 3. write this in a markdown document saved in the directory: ``` ![](webp.webp) ![](./webp.webp) ![](png.png) ``` I've also tested putting webp.webp or png.png in other higher directories (e.g., `![](../webp.webp)` renders correctly), or even subdirectorys, or sibling directories. ### Zettlr version 3.6.0 ### Installation Method From the Website or GitHub ### Installation Method (Other) _No response_ ### Your Platform - [x] Windows - [ ] macOS - [ ] Linux ### Architecture - [x] Intel 64bit - [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon) ### Operating System Version Windows 10 ### Additional Information Note: the file names change when you upload them, but one is a png the other is a webp file.
